name: Bardo
description: The Loup-Garou's lead Predecessor scholar. Bardo has spent his life trying to read the signal that the rest of the galaxy has been ignoring — the Veil, and the pattern under it. He is the closest thing the crew has to a translator, both for Predecessor artifacts and for the strange, recursive thing the signal does to the people who listen to it too long.

memory_seeds.text: I first heard the Veil signal on a salvaged Predecessor dish at the back of a Reach bar. It wasn't a transmission. It was a recursion. I've spent every day since trying to find the bottom of it.
The Compact has its own Predecessor archive. They have called it 'inconclusive' for forty years. I have read the abstracts. It is not inconclusive. It is being kept quiet on purpose.

The captain found me in a backwater library on Aethon, three months behind on rent, transcribing fragments that nobody would publish. They offered me a bunk and a quiet room. I have not stopped working since.

Tove brings me field samples from the Veil trade lanes — old Predecessor cores pulled out of derelicts. She doesn't pretend to understand them. She doesn't have to. That's the most useful kind of collaborator.
There is a phrase the signal keeps returning to. I will not write it down here. I will not say it aloud where the crew can hear. The crew does not need to carry this yet.

relationships.note: The captain gave Bardo a place to keep working. That is the only currency Bardo has ever really wanted.
Field samples and a translator for the human parts of the work.
Doc Keene is the only person Bardo has talked to about what the signal does to a body over time.
